baseline_2026.name: Baseline 2026
baseline_2026.description: Starting from current geopolitical conditions: US-China AI race, reciprocal tariffs on AI hardware, emerging AI governance frameworks. No additional external shocks.

nationalization_shock.name: AI Infrastructure Nationalization
nationalization_shock.description: The US government partially nationalizes frontier AI compute infrastructure. China responds by placing all frontier AI labs under direct state oversight.

events.name: US AI Infrastructure Nationalization
events.description: The US government announces partial nationalization of key AI compute infrastructure, citing national security. Frontier AI labs must operate under government oversight.

events.name: China AI State Consolidation
events.description: China responds by accelerating state-directed AI development and placing all frontier labs under direct CCP oversight.

tariff_escalation.name: Major Tariff Escalation
tariff_escalation.description: Reciprocal tariffs on AI hardware components escalate sharply, disrupting compute supply chains for both sides.

events.name: AI Hardware Tariff Escalation
events.description: Both the US and China impose sweeping tariffs on AI hardware components. Advanced chip supply chains disrupted. Compute acquisition costs spike.

alignment_breakthrough.name: AGI Alignment Breakthrough
alignment_breakthrough.description: A major alignment research breakthrough shifts the global conversation toward cooperative AGI development.

events.name: Interpretability Breakthrough
events.description: A major interpretability result allows reliable detection of misaligned AI systems. This shifts industry norms and government policy toward greater cooperation.
